mentions.note: Value retailer held by travel trust; ideal for discount shopping, held up very nicely today. Not dollar stores but affordable quality.
Owned by Travel Trust; ideal place to shop for value. Held up very nicely today. Cramer finds unexpected treasures every visit; cheap compared to department stores.
Held up very nicely today; ideal place to shop for value. Not dollar stores but cheap goods. Cramer loves the value, finds good deals every visit. Defensive retail play.
Value retailer (TJ Maxx, Marshall's) owned by Travel Trust. Held up very nicely today. Ideal value shopping destination. Not dollar store but affordable quality. Defensive consumer play.
Trust-owned. Ideal value shopping destination; held up very well in weak market. Secular disinflationary play vs. dollar stores. Defensive.
Cramer's long-term favorite in off-price retail; mentioned as beneficiary of same discount-retail thesis driving Ross success. Would love to own both if diversification rules allowed.
Off-price discount model still working because it's about inventory selection/liquidation from full-price retailers, not everyday low pricing; fundamentally different from pure dollar stores.

Best retail performer: 6% SSS beat (vs. 4.1% exp.), 9% revenue growth, HomeGoods +9%, 17¢ EPS beat, raised FY guidance and buyback. Off-price dominance when consumer nervous; fantastic execution; stock rallied 5.7%.
Superb in current environment; can buy distressed inventory from other retailers at pennies; Chapel Trust owns for years; tempted to buy more on decline; should rally but selling instead.
Best-in-class off-price peer; OLLI's comparison; +5% SSS growth, global scale, trading 30x earnings deservedly at premium.
Down 2.6%; off-price apparel retailer hitting speedbump; had been outperformer but now showing consumer pullback.
Discounter cleaning up on excess inventory from troubled retailers, extraordinary numbers across TJ Maxx, Marshalls, HomeGoods—scooping up distressed stock.
MVP identified by 100X; bargain retail business model; value-for-price resonates across income levels; consistent trader-up multiple candidate.
Trade-down retailer (HomeGoods, Marshalls, TJ Maxx) beneficiary of oil shock-induced inflation and financially challenged families moving down; buyable theme if oil stabilizes.
Off-price retailer reporting tomorrow; understandable value model; benefits from rotation to companies making/selling tangible goods cheaply.
